title: Monolithic Modernity
description: Bringing monoliths from alpha back into modern Minecraft!
body:
# Monolithic Modernity
![Image of a monolith top with a poppy in focus, with "Monolithic Modernity" written over it. ](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6690e53557cc84aaae71ffdbe4bcdf427b578519.png)
Did you know that in 1.19 monoliths were techinically possible again? Well, this datapack aims to add them back in the game! (although more commonly than in alpha)
# Description
This pack uses a bug from 1.19 in which, if you multiply the terrain density function by a negative number, it shoots the terrain up into the the sky. Then, with a range choice, I made that terrain much rarer.

Tldr: I made a bug a feature.
# Compatibility
This should be incompatible with any other that touches the sloped_cheese file, but it's fairly easy to make it compatible. For now there's a separate file compatible with [Cascades](https://modrinth.com/datapack/hybrid-beta).
# Credits 
- Special thanks to [Hazel](https://modrinth.com/user/HazelsInferno) for giving me the pack idea while developing her pack, [Seecret Sunday](https://modrinth.com/modpack/seecret-sunday) (Go play it!)
- Thanks also to [29Naybla](https://modrinth.com/user/29Naybla) for helping me test it in the a pack with Cascades. We're also making a modpack called [Box of Snails](https://modrinth.com/modpack/box-of-snails), in which these will be featured. Check it when it's out and come hang out in our [Discord](https://discord.gg/k8Av5tsXQy)!
- And finally, a huge thanks to [DB3K's](https://legacy.curseforge.com/minecraft/modpacks/db3k) discord server. Not only it hugely inspired us, it also allowed a space to develop our projects.
